user_text:
The process begins with the Mail Processing Unit collecting mail from the party. Once the mail is collected, a Mail Clerk sorts the unopened mail. After sorting, the Mail Clerk distributes the mail accordingly. The next step involves the Registry opening the mail. Once opened, the Registry sorts the mail and then registers it in a Mail Register.

Following the registration, the Assistant Registry Manager (ARM) performs a quality check on the mail. At this point, a decision is made. If the mail requires a list of requisitions, the ARM compiles this list and sends it back to the party. This concludes one possible path of the process.

Alternatively, if the mail involves matter details, the ARM captures these details and provides them to the Cashier. The Cashier then takes the applicable fees. After the fees are taken, two activities occur simultaneously. On one hand, the ARM puts the receipt and copies documents into an envelope, then posts these documents to the party. On the other hand, the Cashier captures the party details and prints the Physical Court File. Once both parallel activities are completed, the process paths converge.

Finally, the process concludes after either sending the list of requisitions back to the party or completing the steps involving the matter details and fees.
output_text:
The process begins with the Mail Clerk distributing the mail. After distributing the mail, the Mail Clerk sorts the unopened mail. Once the mail is sorted, the Mail Processing Unit collects the mail from the party. Following this, the Registry opens the mail. After opening the mail, the Registry sorts it. The sorted mail is then registered by the Registry in a Mail Register.

Next, the Assistant Registry Manager (ARM) performs a quality check on the registered mail. After the quality check, a decision is made. If the decision leads to compiling a list of requisitions, the ARM compiles this list and sends it back to the party. This path concludes with the process reaching a decision point again.

Alternatively, if the decision leads to capturing the matter details, the ARM captures these details and provides them to the Cashier. The Cashier then takes the applicable fees. At this point, two activities occur in parallel.

In one parallel path, the ARM puts the receipt and then copies documents into an envelope. Afterward, the ARM posts these documents to the party. This path concludes at a synchronization point.

In the other parallel path, the Cashier captures the party details and then prints the Physical Court File. This path also concludes at the same synchronization point.

Once both parallel paths are completed, the process reaches the decision point again, and then the process ends.
